text:
WHO DECIDES IF YOUR BTC UP BET WINS OR LOSES? HINT: NOT POLYMARKET

Every BTC up or down market, every 5-minute candle people gamble their rent on, all of it has to settle on a number, and that number comes exactly from @PythNetwork

just think about it, you are not really betting against the house, you are betting against whatever Pyth prints the second that candle closes.

and it is not just about polymarket, kalshi up/down runs on it, perps on hyperliquid, coinbase, binance, 

all pulling the same feed 24/7 while everyone stares at the chart and nobody looks at the thing drawing it.

the entire up/down meta, billions in volume, sits on top of an oracle most traders could not even name.

"A single LLM is a tool. A multi-agent architecture is a quant team." - Nicole Königstein

her talk "financial innovation and data analysis with agentic llms" is a full-hour breakdown of building autonomous multi-agent systems for market analysis.

watching this will move you further than everything you did all week 👇

THIS GUY BUILT A FARM OF MACS THAT RUN AI AGENTS 24/7 AND RENTS THEM OUT

just racks of mac minis sitting there, running models while he sleeps, and he rents them out to other people.

the thing is, you don't need a warehouse to do this yourself.

one mac mini m4 does the same thing on your desk. 

it pulls 40W, same as a desk lamp, starts at $599, and runs a local LLM without sending anything to the cloud.

the thing that makes it work is ollama. you just install it, pull a model like Qwen or Deepseek, and it just runs on your own machine. 

no subscription, no monthly fees, your data never leaves the box. 

Since March it runs on mlx too, so on a Mac, it is actually fast, not a slideshow.

so instead of renting compute from some guy, you own the whole thing. the mini pc basically becomes an employee that works for the cost of electricity.

check the best mini PCs and the exact scripts to set this up in the article below 👇

THIS MODDED MAC MINI RUNS AN ENTIRE X CONTENT FARM WHILE ITS OWNER SLEEPS

cooled to run 24/7, it does the job a $1,500 a month ghostwriter does, for around $20

real numbers from one setup: 15,500 followers and 7.7M impressions in four months, plus $2,800 paid out by x directly, before any backend leads.

here is what the machine actually does, stage by stage:

→ the source
instead of recycling the same big accounts, it pulls from bilibili with auto translate on, fresh concepts the western timeline has never seen

→ the extraction
rip the audio from any video, drop it into a whisper bot on telegram, and a 40 minute podcast becomes clean text in minutes

→ the brain
that text goes into claude with one tuned prompt: pull the 5 most counter intuitive insights, rewrite each as a standalone post. ten minutes, five human sounding drafts

→ the magnet
lock the best resource behind a follow and a keyword in the replies. comments spike, the algorithm reads it as heat and pushes you to millions

The takeaway is simple: stop writing from scratch and let the machine do the boring part.

THIS 40-MINUTE MIT LECTURE WILL CHANGE HOW YOU THINK ABOUT AI

Paul Liang just dropped the intro to "How to AI Almost Anything" 

The era of just typing text into a chatbox is officially over.

In this lecture, he reveals how to build multi-sensory agents that go beyond screens: processing touch, smell, and taking real-world actions.

Don't put it off - this is how your future will look

A WEBCAM ON A 3D PRINTED TURRET CAN ALREADY LOCK ONTO A MOVING DRONE IN REAL TIME

one logitech cam paired with a vision model at 30 fps and stepper motors that keep the crosshair locked on the drone as it flies.

now scale that: put one on every balcony and rooftop in a city, each node watching its slice of the sky and reporting the second it sees a drone, position and heading streamed to one map in real time.

you don't need expensive radar to know something is coming. you need thousands of cheap eyes that never blink.

and here is what makes it spread on its own. the state pays every owner in crypto for every confirmed detection. your rooftop becomes a sensor that earns.

a decentralized early warning grid, built from $50 parts and code that an AI agent can write today. 

the hardware is already here - the only missing piece is the network on top.

THIS 41-MINUTE TALK FROM THE HEAD OF CLAUDE CODE WILL TEACH YOU EVERYTHING ABOUT AI LOOPS

@bcherny hasn't written a line of code by hand in 8 months. another agent does the prompting for him now.

this is the shift from prompt engineering to loop engineering, and he breaks down exactly how it works.

what stuck with me after seeing it:
→ stop prompting by hand, start orchestrating loops that run on their own 
→ the bottleneck is no longer code. it is having good ideas worth building 
→ every token has a real cost, so the question becomes what the output is actually worth

if you are still writing code by hand or babysitting every output, this is the part you do not want to miss.

watch it 👇

THIS GUY STOPPED PROMPTING CLAUDE. HE BUILT A DEV TEAM OF AGENTS THAT LOOPS WITHOUT HIM

Your AI coding agent runs once and hands back whatever comes out, bugs and all. 

So he built a team that loops on its own: building, testing, and fixing until it is perfect.

two specialists in claude code:

→ the builder I writes and fixes code.
→ the checker I runs tests and reports what failed.

you have to always keep them separate. 

Because an agent that checks its own work never catches its blind spots.

An orchestrator chains them into a loop. 

builder ships, checker runs. all green, it stops. 

if something failed, it goes back, gets fixed, and runs again until it passes.

plus stop rules, so it never burns all your tokens: max five cycles, and it halts if a fix breaks something that was passing.
 
A full step-by-step guide with the exact commands is right below 👇

THE GUY WHO BUILT CLAUDE CODE JUST SAID HE STOPPED PROMPTING IT

Boris Cherny: "i no longer prompt claude. i have loops running that prompt claude themselves and decide what to do next. my job is to write these loops."

for years the top AI skill was writing good prompts.

the problem is you are stuck in endless back and forth, holding the agent's hand the whole time.

the new skill is loop engineering.

a loop is a system that prompts the agent for you.

you give it one goal, and it runs on its own. it finds the work, does it, checks it, remembers what it finished, and goes again until the task is actually done.

the reason it works is simple.

clear completion criteria instead of "build the feature".

independent verification, so the agent checking the code is not the same one that wrote it.

hard limits on iterations and tokens, so it never loops forever or burns your money.

according to Cherny, a big chunk of the code inside Anthropic is already written this way.

engineers stopped writing code and even stopped managing agents. they design the process instead.

you stop steering the agent and start building the thing that steers it.

full breakdown below 👇

THIS DEVELOPER WIRED THE X API INTO CLAUDE AND NOW IT UPLOADS HIS AI VIDEOS 24/7

In the video below, he explains how to fully automate the manual uploading grind.

After setting up Claude to push automatic videos to Instagram and TikTok (00:14), he shows how to let the AI take over Twitter completely.

now the system authenticates itself and automatically publishes posts while he does basically nothing.

and here is exactly what he breaks down in the tutorial:

→ going to the X developer console to spin up a production app (01:21) 
→ generating the consumer keys and access tokens 
→ dropping the credentials into a blank secrets.json file (04:55) 
→ running a script so Claude reads the keys and pushes the video live (06:40)

total cost: $0.01 per post 
total time: zero manual work after setup

why it makes sense? 

If you are already generating automatic videos, uploading by hand might be a bottleneck. Building the system once and letting it run is a way easier thing.

HERMES DESKTOP JUST FIXED THE WORST PART OF AI AGENTS

Claude Code forgets everything the second you close the terminal.

Hermes does the opposite. 

the more you use it, the smarter it gets about YOU

→ persistent memory across every single session
→ builds a library of reusable skills you can pull whenever you want
→ memory carries over no matter how much you talk to it
→ gets stronger over time instead of resetting

they are calling it the world's #1 personal agent that improves the more you use it.

read below to see exactly how it works 👇

THIS GUY GAVE CLAUDE CODE HIS TWITTER COOKIES AND NOW IT POSTS AS HIM 24/7

he got tired of grinding his account by hand, so instead of hiring a ghostwriter, he just built a system once and let it take over completely. 

now the thing tweets, replies, reposts, and slides into DMs in his exact voice while he does basically nothing.

and here is how he actually pulled it off:

→ exported every past tweet, dumped them into a JSON file, fed it to Claude Code as a style brain so it writes in his voice

→ wired in an Apify actor that scrapes the top 50 AI accounts and pulls their last 24h of tweets, so Claude always has fresh news to post about

→ exported the cookies from his logged-in browser session and handed them to Claude Code, which spins up its own browser through the playwright mcp and logs in as him

→ wrote one long behavior script: when to reply, when to repost, when to tweet, when to DM, all randomized so it reads like a human

→ shipped the whole thing to a VPS so it runs around the clock with almost zero screen time

no blank page. no luck. only a system posting in his voice while he sleeps.

i broke down the full automation stack in my article below ↓
https://t.co/H3ViBNOE4i

still cheap to follow him, what do you think guys?

STOP PAYING $200 A MONTH FOR AI. YOU CAN BUILD A LOCAL AI SERVER FOR $249 AND RUN IT FOR $3/MONTH.

Most people are quietly bleeding $100–$300 a month on ChatGPT Plus, Claude, Cursor, and API costs without thinking twice.

The hardware market has completely shifted.

Right now, an NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano Super handles 80% of daily AI work - coding, document analysis, n8n automations - instantly.

This video reveals the exact setup to run models like Llama 3.2 locally and privately.

here's how you do it in one afternoon:

→ get an NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano 
→ flash JetPack to a micro SD card 
→ clone the OS to an SSD using shell scripts for 10x read/write speeds 
→ install Ollama with a single curl command 
→ change your code's base_url to localhost

total cost: $249 one-time 
monthly cost: $3 for electricity

why it makes sense? 

If you pay $100/month in cloud APIs, this pays for itself in 2.5 months. After that, every interaction is free. Plus, zero data leaves your machine. Perfect for NDAs and client work.

Everything you need to build this is right below 👇

Realtors think he’s running a premium design agency. In reality, he’s one guy in his bedroom making $8,400 a month using Claude Design

Max used to be an ordinary frontend developer. 

He spent his days trapped in the pixel-pushing grind, writing endless lines of CSS, and assuming that making big money required a massive team and months of coding.

But one evening, a friend showed him Claude Design, and it completely changed the game.

So, when a high-ticket California realtor panic-texted him needing a luxury villa website live by tomorrow morning, most devs would have laughed. Max just replied: "Say less."

Instead of coding from scratch, he handed a brief to Claude Design. Within minutes, the AI generated a flawless, high-ticket design system: a moody dark theme, perfect typography, and exact spacing.

By morning, the site was live, looking like a top-tier agency spent a month on it. Max billed $1,500 for a single evening. 

Today, he runs this exact AI playbook to quietly pull in $8,400 a month on his own terms.

claude code has a mode that refuses to write code until it interviews you first, it is the only one i use now

it is called plan mode

you do not need a clear path to start, 

you drop a light prompt, and Claude Code starts interviewing you instead of dumping code

in this video the guy asks for a low poly pitfall with jump over pits and barriers, and watch how it walks him through it:

→ what visual style 
→ what stack and platform 
→ 2d or 3d, what camera

And you are never locked into the suggested answers, the options did not fit, so he just typed his own spec

few rounds later you get a full plan you can sanity check before a single line is written

then the clean finish:

→ accept the plan 
→ clear context 
→ build in auto mode

result was a clean working pitfall, no extra features nobody asked for

this is hack 7 of 32 from the article below 👇

A TRADER MADE $118.2K IN A MONTH BY EXPLOITING POLYMARKET LATENCY

He noticed inefficiencies in new prediction markets and decided to build his own automated latency arbitrage terminal.

Right now, he is in a live-testing phase of the architecture on a Wi-Fi system,

and here is the exact stack and range of tools it uses:

- Custom Chainlink data feed
- Polymarket official trading feed
- Live algorithm with a per-session monitor for trades, wins, and win rates
- Covering markets: 5-minute Bitcoin and Ethereum Up or Down contracts

It pulls real-time pricing metrics and compares them to detect data dislocation between the custom feed and Polymarket's feed.

The system exploits these price lags to auto-execute trades, hitting 64,910 predictions.

results:
➡ Bitcoin Up or Down on Apr 12: bet $5,475.58 → won $10,651.07 
➡ Bitcoin Up or Down on Apr 15: bet $2,109.62 → won $5,082.11 
➡ Ethereum Up or Down on Mar 26: bet $459.23 → won $1,933.46

THIS KID IS ATTEMPTING TO TURN $100 INTO $1,000 USING A FULLY AUTOMATED AI BOT

He set a public challenge to turn a $100 deposit into $1,000 using only an autonomous trading bot.

Right now, he is sitting at $496.22 in pure realized profit.

But it wasn't a straight line up.

Mid-challenge, he realized his script had a critical bug that kept firing fake entry signals and bleeding capital.

 Instead of quitting, he completely nuked the architecture.

Here is the current state of the rebuild:
→ rewrote the entire execution logic from scratch using AI 
→ patched the signal bugs to stop unnecessary volume 
→ the updated model pulled a 25% ROI in just a single week

Currently on Day 19 and has exactly 11 days left on the clock to hit that $1,000 target.

what do you think, will he succeed?

HOW MY $20/MONTH HERMES-AGENT REPLACED A $1K+ PERSONAL ASSISTANT

runs 24/7 on a Mac mini, talks to you through telegram like a person

what it does: finance tracking, research, bookmarking, travel planning, basically anything you'd hire a PA for

how it works:
→ install as a desktop app on a Mac mini or VPS, no terminal needed
→ connect it to telegram through @botfather, then just text it from your phone
→ runs on gpt-5.4/5.5 under the hood
→ self-improving loop: the longer you talk to it, the more it learns about you and the better it gets at the job

no salary, no hours, no onboarding.

just a subscription and a machine you probably already own.

There's one setup decision that actually matters: how it touches your machine.
